series_metadata.series_description: Records include correspondence, inspection reports, complaint reports, Warning Letters, Notices of Deficiency, Notices of Violation, Emergency Orders, Consent Orders, Judgement Decrees, Summary Judgements, penalty assessments and penalty correspondence, hearing transcripts, and associated supporting documents.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Keep for 5 years unless a licensee or permittee is involved in litigation with the Division, in which case records will be kept for two years after all administrative, civil or criminal action is complete. Then destroy., Ohio Civil Rule of Procedure 41(A) ; Electronic: Keep for 5 years unless a licensee or permittee is involved in litigation with the Division, in which case records will be kept for two years after all administrative, civil or criminal action is complete. Then destroy., Ohio Civil Rule of Procedure 41(A), Paper: Trash ; Electronic: Delete
